FULL CONTENT:

## Upcoming Events

# Renew & Revive Spiritual Support Group Meetings

![Renew and Revive Spiritual Support Group meetings to discuss mental health and spiritual well-being.](data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB3aWR0aD0iMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIxIiB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciPjwvc3ZnPg==)

We regularly host events online and offline to meet, share, and discuss various topics pertaining to mental health and spiritual well-being. Take a look at our calendar of events below and sign up here if you want to ensure you get invites to all upcoming events!

**Series: Winning the Fight Against Anxiety**

It’s astonishing that worry and anxiety comes in such various forms. Living with anxiety is so common, many people do not realize that we need not suffer from it so immensely. Join us and learn the different ways anxiety is experienced and how we can effectively battle it with God’s truth.

**Social Anxiety**  
Wednesday, July 30, 2025, 7:30pm – 8:30pm PT  
[Sign Up Here](https://www.oakhealthfoundation.org/fullyhealth/renew-revive/#renew-revive-form)

**Anticipatory Anxiety**  
Wednesday, August 6, 2025, 7:30pm – 8:30pm PT  
[Sign Up Here](https://www.oakhealthfoundation.org/fullyhealth/renew-revive/#renew-revive-form)

**OCD**  
Wednesday, August 13, 2025, 7:30pm – 8:30pm PT  
[Sign Up Here](https://www.oakhealthfoundation.org/fullyhealth/renew-revive/#renew-revive-form)

**What is Agoraphobia?**  
Wednesday, August 27, 2025, 7:30pm – 8:30pm PT  
[Sign Up Here](https://www.oakhealthfoundation.org/fullyhealth/renew-revive/#renew-revive-form)

## Subscribe to our newsletter to stay updated with what Oak Health Foundation is doing!

We’ll send out periodic updates about what the foundation is doing and how you can get involved.

[Click Here to Read Previous Newsletters](/newsletters/)

Laguna Hills, California | [connect@oakhf.org](mailto:connect@oakhf.org)

### Renew & Revive Spiritual Group Sign Up

This Renew & Revive (R&R) group is a program established by the team at Fully Health to provide a comprehensive approach to address all our needs – physical, mental, and spiritual. Through attending these complimentary group sessions, you will learn about the good news of Jesus Christ and how God will intervene during our journey toward achieving healing and greatest potential.

All individuals 18+ are welcome to attend! You do not need to be a Fully Health client or patient to attend. This service is free of charge. Please note, these sessions are not a clinical service. You may come and go as you like. We would be happy to have you whenever you may be available to join!

Wednesdays 7:30 – 8:30 pm on Zoom

**What to expect from R&R**  
– To deeply understand, believe, and enjoy the Gospel, and know who I am  
– To be able to have any question answered without judgment. For example, “Why am I struggling so much even though I have faith?” and “If I seek psychiatric treatment, does that mean my faith is small?”  
– To experience the ability to fight the spiritual battle in life, especially regarding mental health struggles around me  
– If Jesus is the Christ who solved my fundamental spiritual problem that led to all the “bad fruits” of life, then He is the answer to any other problem (mental and/or physical) that I may face on this earth.

--- SOURCE 4: Healing Connections Peer Support Group ---
URL: https://mhaw.org/programs/online-peer-support-groups/

SUMMARY:
Missing: spirituality well- being

FULL CONTENT:
*![Icon](https://mhaw.org/wp-content/themes/socialwelfare/images/top-bar-icon-new.png)* To Advocate. To Educate. To Empower. Together.

![Icon](https://mhaw.org/wp-content/themes/socialwelfare/images/top-bar-icon-new.png)

Mental Health Helpline 631.471.7242 ext. 2

helpline@mhaw.org

![](https://mhaw.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/mhawhdLogo-5.png)

### Healing Connections Peer Support Group

Welcome to Healing Connections a support group for and by people who experience emotional distress, voices and visions, extreme and altered states of consciousness, labels of psychiatry, substance use, and trauma/trauma responses.

Our Healing Connections Peer Support Group is **currently taking referrals. Please call 631-471-7242 ext. 1330 to add your name to the list of interested individuals and to be contacted when the group re-opens for new members.**

**Healing Connections Peer Support Group Agreement**

**Etiquette**

We agree to be respectful toward all group members in order to maintain the safety of our group. We honor that group members will share aspects of their personal identity that are meaningful to them and we are expected to be supportive of this sharing. Aspects of identity that might be discussed include race, ethnicity, language, nationality, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, ability, size, socioeconomic status, and more.

**Confidentiality**

Each of our privacy is important and respected. The recording feature has been restricted from any of our offered Peer Support Services. We ask that each of us uphold privacy and anything that is shared in our support group, stay within that group. We ask that we do not take what is shared in one group into any other group. Our group decides the limits to maintaining privacy, and there is transparency at all times around any limits that are identified.

**Check-in: Introductions, talk time, comfort agreement, confidentiality**

We will all say our name and share if we would like to talk. As stated in our Guiding Principles talking is completely voluntary, if one chooses to only listen that is more than okay, just let us know during this check-in. We respect that we each have the right to change our mind to talk at any point. We will review comfort agreement and confidentiality.

**Sharing**

***Be sure everyone has a chance to talk******.*** Some people are naturally more talkative than others. We can ask questions to offer opportunity for quiet group members to share. It’s also crucial if we are more vocal to be mindful that everyone has a chance to contribute.

**Chat Feature**

Due to expressed safety concerns the chat feature is off. If anyone would like to exchange contact information, please let us know. If you would like to connect with us offline with questions, suggestions, or comments, email [healingconnections@mhaw.org](mailto:healingconnections@mhaw.org)

**GUIDING PRINCIPLES**

Our Guiding Principles are adapted from the following resources: Western Massachusetts Peer Network’s Declaration of Peer Roles and Shery Mead’s Intentional Peer Support (IPS)

**Healing Connections Peer Support Groups** were created for and by people who experience emotional distress, voices and visions, extreme and altered states of consciousness, labels of psychiatric diagnosis, substance use, and trauma/trauma responses. We come together to share information, hope, joys, fears, successes, and hardships in the most safe space we co-create to mutually support, heal, and grow together. We all are collectively responsible for the comfort of our group!

Healing Connections facilitators are trained Peer Specialist, who have their own experience with emotional distress. The facilitators are there to guide the support group and offer peer support.

**Healing Connections Peer Support Line**

For information on our Peer Support Line [click here](http://mhaw.org/programs/peer-support-line/) or call us at 631-471-7242 ext. 1217 Tuesday to Friday from 10:00am to 1:00pm for one-to-one telephone mutual support.

###### STAY IN TOUCH

631.471.7242

You may unsubscribe via the link found at the bottom of every email. (See our [Email Privacy Policy](http://www.constantcontact.com/legal/privacy-statement) for details.) ### Frequently Asked Questions

* * *

#### Why Join a Support Group?

Support groups offer a unique opportunity to connect with others who share similar experiences. They help reduce isolation and loneliness, provide a sense of community, and offer practical advice and support. Here are some key benefits of joining a support group:

*   **Shared Experiences:** Find comfort in knowing you are not alone in your struggles.
*   **Emotional Support:** Receive and provide encouragement and empathy.
*   **Practical Advice:** Gain insights and tips from others who have faced similar challenges.
*   **Personal Growth:** Enhance your coping skills and resilience.

* * *

#### Who Should Use Support Groups?

Support groups are open to anyone looking for support. They often focus on specific topics such as depression, anxiety, family issues, or grief. It's important to find a group that feels right for you. If the first group you join doesn't meet your needs, don't hesitate to try another one.

* * *

#### Why use HeyPeers for Support Groups?

At HeyPeers, we provide a platform where multiple organizations offer a wide range of support groups. Many of these groups are offered for free by non-profits dedicated to supporting your mental health and well-being. Additionally, we feature HeyPeers-certified meetings conducted by verified certified peer specialists and independent peer specialists who offer their services via our platform. If you are feeling overwhelmed or isolated, our platform provides the support you need to empower yourself and take control of your mental health.

One of the biggest reasons HeyPeers stands out is that only certified peer specialists can lead support groups on our platform. This ensures better quality of support and follows the right protocols to make sure the support group is a safe place. Unlike random peer-led support groups, our certified specialists are trained to provide proper guidance without suggesting potentially harmful medications or practices.

* * *

#### Is HeyPeers a Safe Place for Mental Health Issues?

Yes, HeyPeers is designed to be a safe and supportive environment for addressing mental health issues. On HeyPeers, support groups can be joined anonymously, allowing you to participate without revealing your identity. We also have mechanisms in place to stop any inappropriate behavior, ensuring that the support group remains a safe and respectful space for all participants. Our certified peer specialists are trained to provide proper guidance and follow protocols that promote safety and well-being in every session.

* * *

#### Types of Support Groups Available on HeyPeers

*   **Free Support Groups:** Offered by various non-profit organizations, these groups cover a range of topics and provide free access to support.
*   **HeyPeers-Certified Meetings:** Conducted by verified certified peer specialists, these paid meetings ensure high-quality, professional support.
*   **Independent Peer Specialists:** Individual peer specialists offering personalized support services through our platform. These specialists provide one-on-one sessions and create specific topics for personalized meetings. Users can register for these paid services to receive targeted support.
*   **Team-Led Groups:** Some organizations offer team-led groups with more than one group leader, providing diverse perspectives and enhanced support.

* * *

#### Can I Access Support Groups Online?

Yes, HeyPeers offers online support groups, allowing you to connect with others from the comfort of your home. Our online communities and discussion boards provide additional ways to stay connected and supported, especially if local groups are not available.

* * *

#### What Topics Do HeyPeers Support Groups Cover?

HeyPeers provides a platform for various organizations to offer support groups covering a wide range of topics to address diverse mental health and well-being needs. In addition to HeyPeers-certified support groups, our platform hosts groups focused on:

*   **[Addiction](https://www.heypeers.com/support_groups/addiction)**
*   **[Anxiety](https://www.heypeers.com/support_groups/anxiety)**
*   **[Bipolar](https://www.heypeers.com/support_groups/bipolar)**

DESCRIPTION: A diverse group of people gathers in a circular arrangement around a central laptop displaying virtual support icons, symbolizing online mental health support, spirituality, and well-being.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Digital platforms like HeyPeers offer a variety of support groups led by certified specialists, enabling individuals to connect and find solace in shared experiences from anywhere.
Programs such as Oak Health's Renew & Revive group explore the integration of faith and mental wellness, helping participants navigate mental health challenges through spiritual insights.

Participation in faith-based support groups fosters emotional resilience, reduces isolation, and promotes healing, as evidenced by the nurturing environments created in groups like Healing Connections.
